title: Frequent Flyer Needs Therapy | #shorts
full_text: Corey, I'm the world's leading almost therapist. Whatever your problem is, I can solve it. Tell me right now. Go. >> I am on a plane twice a week, every week, and I can't stand it anymore. I don't want to be treated like all the regular people who fly once in a while. >> Oh, so you think you're special? It >> You don't understand. There's all these levels. There's like gold and silver and platinum and titanium and uranium and enriched uranium. And it it takes so long to to become like important. >> Oh, so your issue is you think you're very important and the world doesn't see it. >> Clearly, I'm more important if I fly same airline every week. I >> I'm I'm noticing a sense of elevated self-importance. I I think that that could be the root of the problem here. >> I have to be important at something. Okay. I gave up trying to be a star in show business. Now I just want to be a star in status on airplanes. It just seems very doable. >> Do you have status on any? >> I have executive platinum on American Airlines. My favorite. >> Okay. >> But I I just got it and now I have to start working on it for next year. For next year. >> So you think you should just get it once and that's for life? Like for life? Everybody should just like bend down and like lay down at your feet because you're a star. >> I'm going to die soon. They could just give it to me until I die. Well, you're going to die sooner if you keep this attitude up. Like, they know which planes got problems. They're going to put you in those planes. >> Do I complain about anything but airlines? That's all I complain. Everything else is great in my life. >> Your husband? >> Terrific. >> Your husband's terrific. >> Terrific. >> Your kids? >> Pretty good. >> Are you on a lot of drugs right now?
